topic: A person finds out their article was published under someone else's name
raw_output:
[story 1]

Elara stared at the screen, her breath catching in her throat. The headline, the lead paragraph—it all belonged to Julian Vance. Her carefully crafted analysis, the one she’d poured weeks into, was now branded as his. She leaned back in her worn office chair, the springs groaning under her weight, and slowly, deliberately, she reread the text. Every sentence felt alien, yet undeniably hers. A cold prickle traced a path down her spine. She reached out, her fingers trembling slightly, and touched the words as if expecting them to dissolve. Who was Julian Vance? She hadn't even seen his name attached to this piece. A slow, disbelieving shake ran through her shoulders.

[story 2]

"This can't be right," Marcus muttered, running a hand through his already disheveled hair. He kept refreshing the publication website, the screen illuminating the sudden starkness of his features. The article, his deeply personal essay on urban decay, was undeniably online, but the byline screamed another name. He stood up abruptly, pacing the narrow confines of his apartment. His jaw tightened, a knot forming deep in his chest. He walked over to the window, looking out at the indifferent city lights, the vastness amplifying the sheer absurdity of the situation. He squeezed his eyes shut, trying to force the impossible reality to shift.

[story 3]

The coffee cup slipped from Liam’s grasp, splashing dark liquid across the keyboard. He froze, his hand hovering above the keys, his gaze fixed on the published piece. The font, the cadence, the very argument he had so painstakingly constructed—it was all there, flawlessly presented, but the attribution was wrong. A hollow feeling settled in his stomach, heavy and undeniable. He leaned forward, resting his elbows on the desk, his gaze unfocused as he tried to reconcile the reality before him with the memory of his own labor. He let out a quiet, ragged exhale, the sound barely audible over the hum of the computer.
